question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadWithRetry(FileID key, long currentTimestamp, SystemFile systemFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the AtomicInteger attempt counter in loadWithRetry() to cause a denial-of-service by triggering 10 consecutive DB reads (maxRetries=9) per request, exhausting DB connection pools and causing other historical queries to time out and return stale cached data?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: load(FileID fileId, long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that load() logs at INFO level when updating the cache (log.info('Updating cache for key {}')) to infer cache miss patterns and determine which historical timestamps have not yet been queried, then use this timing side-channel to reconstruct the transaction history access pattern of other users?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: load(FileID fileId, long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CacheKey record (FileID fileId, long timestamp) to cause cache key collisions by supplying a FileID with different shard/realm/num combinations that hash to the same value, causing the wrong system file to be returned for a historical query and reorganizing the apparent file update history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: CacheKey.toString()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CacheKey.toString() method (which uses EntityId.of(fileId.shardNum(), fileId.realmNum(), fileId.fileNum())) to cause log injection by crafting a FileID with special characters in its numeric fields, polluting the log with misleading entries that make the transaction history appear to have been accessed at different timestamps?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: load(FileID fileId, long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that genesisNetworkProperties is always returned for the networkPropertyFile without any timestamp check, causing all historical queries for network properties to return the genesis version regardless of when the query is for, making the transaction history appear to always use genesis-era network properties?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: load(FileID fileId, long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the early return for genesisNetworkProperties (if (genesisNetworkProperties.fileId().equals(fileId)) return genesisNetworkProperties) to cause a discrepancy between the network properties used for EVM execution and those recorded in the transaction history, making it appear that different network rules were applied than were actually in effect?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that loadAll() uses Collectors.toMap() with Function.identity() as the value mapper, so that if two SystemFile entries have the same genesisFile().fileId(), the second silently overwrites the first in the map, causing one system file type to be permanently inaccessible and its historical queries to always return the wrong file?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that loadAll() is lazily initialized and called only once, so that if the DB state changes after initialization (e.g., a new system file version is added), the systemFiles map is never refreshed and all subsequent historical queries return stale genesis file references, distorting the transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: load(FileID fileId, long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of authentication or authorization checks in load() to query any system file (fee schedules, exchange rates, throttle definitions, address book) at any historical timestamp, enumerating the full history of system file changes and using this to reconstruct the transaction history of the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: load(FileID fileId, long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that load() does not validate whether the consensusTimestamp is within a reasonable range (e.g., not before genesis or after the current block), allowing queries for arbitrary historical timestamps that may return genesis files and make the transaction history appear to use genesis-era parameters for any time period?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadWithRetry(FileID key, long currentTimestamp, SystemFile systemFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that loadWithRetry() uses fileDataRepository.getFileAtTimestamp(fileId, nanoSeconds.get()) without any rate limiting, allowing them to trigger thousands of DB reads per second by sending many concurrent requests with different timestamps, causing DB exhaustion and forcing other queries to return stale cached data that misrepresents the transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: load(FileID fileId, long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the exchangeRatesCacheManager uses a different TTL or eviction policy than the defaultSystemFileCacheManager, causing exchange rate or fee schedule entries to be evicted at different times than other system files, creating temporal inconsistencies in the historical data served to different users?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: load(FileID fileId, long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that load() does not check whether the returned File's fileId matches the requested fileId, allowing a scenario where the DB returns a file with a different fileId than requested (due to a DB inconsistency), causing the wrong system file to be cached and served for historical queries?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadWithRetry(FileID key, long currentTimestamp, SystemFile systemFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that File.newBuilder().contents(bytes).fileId(key).build() in loadWithRetry() always uses the requested key as the fileId (not the DB entry's fileId), causing a mismatch between the file's actual identity and its cached identity, which could be exploited to serve one system file's contents under another file's ID?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: load(FileID fileId, long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the cache is populated with cache.put(cacheKey, result) only after a successful DB load, but the cache is checked with cache.get(cacheKey, File.class) before the DB load, creating a window where concurrent requests all miss the cache and all attempt DB loads, potentially causing inconsistent versions to be stored?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isSystemFile(FileID fileId)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit isSystemFile() to enumerate all valid system file IDs by probing different FileID values, then use this knowledge to craft targeted historical queries that return specific system file versions and reconstruct the full transaction history of system file updates?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: load(FileID fileId, long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that load() uses cacheManager.getCache(CACHE_NAME) with a shared cache name across different file types, causing cache entries for different system files to potentially collide if the CacheKey hash/equals implementation has a flaw, returning the wrong file type for a historical query?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadWithRetry(FileID key, long currentTimestamp, SystemFile systemFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the codec field in SystemFile can be null (for hapiPermissionFile and genesisNetworkProperties), causing loadWithRetry() to skip codec.parse() validation and accept any byte sequence as valid file contents, potentially allowing a corrupted or manipulated file to be returned as the authoritative historical version?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: load(FileID fileId, long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that load() does not verify the integrity (e.g., hash or signature) of the file contents returned from the DB, allowing a scenario where a DB entry with tampered bytes passes the codec.parse() check (if the tampered bytes are still valid protobuf) and is served as the authoritative historical system file?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: loadAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that loadAll() initializes the address book files (addressBookFile101, addressBookFile102) with a mock address book (createMockAddressBook()), causing all historical queries for the address book to return a mock with a single hardcoded node (nodeId=3, IP=127.0.0.3), making the transaction history appear to have always used a single-node network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: createMockAddressBook()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that createMockAddressBook() uses a hardcoded nodeId=3 and IP=127.0.0.3, causing all historical address book queries to return a mock that does not reflect the actual network topology, making it impossible to verify which nodes processed historical transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: load(FileID fileId, long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that load() for the address book files (addressBookFile101, addressBookFile102) falls through to loadFromDB() and loadWithRetry(), which may return the mock address book as the genesis file if no DB entry exists, causing historical queries to always show the mock topology regardless of the actual historical address book?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: maxExpiry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxExpiry() uses Instant.now().getEpochSecond() + maxLifetime to set the expiration of genesis files, causing files created at different times to have different expiration values, which could lead to inconsistent behavior when the expiration is checked elsewhere and make the transaction history appear to use files with different validity periods?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: load(EntityId entityId, Bytes contents)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the private load(EntityId, Bytes) method always sets deleted(false) and expirationSecond(maxExpiry()) on genesis files, causing historical queries to return files that appear to be active and non-deleted even for timestamps when the file should have been expired or deleted, distorting the transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: load(FileID fileId, long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that load() does not distinguish between a file that was deleted at the queried timestamp and one that was never created, causing both cases to return the genesis file and making the transaction history appear to use genesis-era parameters even for periods when the file was deleted?
